# **Engineering**

## **Structural Mechanics**

* Force is a push or pull on an object, governed by Newton's L1 Laws of Motion | Physics Axioms | 0.98  
* Stress is force per unit area, an L0 measurable in material testing | Materials Science | 0.94  
* Strain is deformation divided by original dimension | Materials Science | 0.93  
* Young's modulus measures material stiffness, representing an L1 constant for specific L0 materials | Elasticity | 0.92  
* Ultimate tensile strength is the maximum stress a material can withstand before failure | Materials Science | 0.91  
* Shear stress acts parallel to a surface | Mechanics | 0.92  
* Bending moment causes curvature in beams | Structural Analysis | 0.91  
* Factor of safety is design load divided by allowable load, an L3 heuristic for risk management | Pedagogy | 0.93

## **Materials Science**

* Steel is an alloy of iron and carbon | Metallurgy | 0.95  
* Aluminum has a high strength-to-weight ratio | Aerospace Materials | 0.93  
* Hardness measures resistance to deformation | Material Properties | 0.93  
* Toughness is the ability of a material to absorb energy before failure | Material Properties | 0.91  
* Corrosion is the chemical degradation of material, a dependency on Chemistry L1 redox reactions | Cross-Domain | 0.92  
* Polymers are long-chain molecules with repeating units, a concept shared with Biochemistry | Cross-Domain | 0.94

## **Electrical Systems and Computing**

* Voltage is electrical potential difference, an L0 measurement | Electricity | 0.95  
* Current is the flow of charge per unit time | Electricity | 0.95  
* Ohm's law (![][image1]) is a foundational L1 axiom for all electrical engineering | Physics Axioms | 0.96  
* Power dissipation in a resistor (![][image2]) represents a conversion of signal to heat (entropy) | Information Theory | 0.93  
* The Von Neumann Architecture provides the L1 structural blueprint for most History of Computing milestones | Computing | 0.97  
* Feedback Loops are the primary mechanism for control systems and Kitbash homeostatic regulation | Information Theory | 0.95

## **Fluid Mechanics and Aerodynamics**

* Pressure is force per unit area in fluids | Fluid Mechanics | 0.95  
* Bernoulli's principle relates pressure and velocity, providing an L1 rule for aerodynamic L0 observations | Aerodynamics | 0.92  
* Laminar flow occurs in smooth parallel layers, while Turbulent flow is chaotic | Fluid Dynamics | 0.93  
* Reynolds number predicts the flow regime, an L3 heuristic used to bridge L1 equations and L0 data | Philosophy of Science | 0.91  
* Buoyancy is an upward force from displaced fluid, an L1 rule derived from Archimedes' Principle | Physics Axioms | 0.94
